g.data-narration: Welcome to Java Packages and Member Access - the foundation of encapsulation and proper class design.
g.text: Packages And Member Access in Java

g.data-narration: Packages are namespaces that group related classes and interfaces, like folders organizing files on a computer.
g.text: A package is a namespace that groups related classes and interfaces.
Think of it like a folder organizing related files on your computer.

g.data-narration: Packages help organize large applications, avoid naming conflicts, and control access to your classes and members.
g.text: • Organize large-scale applications into logical groups
• Control access to classes and members

g.data-narration: A package declaration must be the first statement in a Java file, defining which package the class belongs to.
g.text: Example: package com.mypackage; (must be first line!)

g.data-narration: Java has four access modifiers that control visibility: public, private, protected, and default (package-private).
The public modifier makes members accessible from any class in any package - least restrictive access level.
g.text: Makes a member accessible from any class in any package.
Least restrictive - use for methods that need to be part of public API.

g.data-narration: The private modifier restricts access to within the same class only - most restrictive access level for encapsulation.
g.text: Makes a member accessible only within the same class.
Most restrictive - essential for encapsulation! Hides internal implementation.

g.data-narration: Protected members are accessible within the same package AND to subclasses in different packages.
g.text: Accessible within same package AND subclasses in different packages.
Useful for inheritance - allows subclasses to access inherited members.

g.data-narration: Default (package-private) access means members are only visible within the same package - no keyword needed.
g.text: Accessible only within the same package. No keyword needed!
If you don't specify any modifier, that's the default. Used for package-level utilities.

g.data-narration: This table shows which access levels allow visibility: same class, same package, subclasses in different packages, and anywhere.
Import statements bring classes into scope: single class import or wildcard import for all classes in a package.
Static import brings static members directly into scope without class name, useful for constants and utility methods.
g.text: Imports static members of a class directly into scope.

g.data-narration: Encapsulation bundles data with methods while restricting access - the foundation of data hiding and secure class design.
g.text: Bundling data and methods while restricting direct access to some components.
Hides implementation details - users interact through public methods (getters/setters).

g.data-narration: Here's a complete example showing all four access modifiers in one class and what each means.
g.text: private int b; // same class only
protected int c; // same pkg + subclasses
• a = public → accessible from any class

g.data-narration: Remember: private less than default less than protected less than public. This is the visibility hierarchy for exams!
g.text: • Top-level classes: public or default only
• Protected: subclass access in diff package
